commit.message:
feat(eu): implement instruction lowering and type system for Phase 4.3

- Add lower.rs module for naga IR to Intel EU instruction translation
  * LoweringContext for managing compilation state and register allocation
  * Binary arithmetic operation lowering (Add, Subtract, Multiply, Divide)
  * Support for all Intel GPU generations (Gen8+)

- Add types.rs module for EU type system integration
  * EUTypeInfo for scalar and vector type representation
  * Type conversion from naga IR to EU register/data types

- Export new types module in eu/mod.rs

This implements the core instruction lowering pipeline required for
Phase 4.3 (GPU Command Submission), enabling translation of platform-
independent shader code to GPU-specific machine instructions.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Adaptive FPS camera: 320x240, starts at 8fps, auto-tunes 2-15fps

- Reduced resolution to 320x240 and JPEG quality to 18 for tiny frames
- Starts at 8 FPS, measures push duration and drop rate every 30 frames
- Scales down if drops > 30% or push time > 80% of interval
- Scales up if drops < 5% and push time < 40% of interval
- Uses recursive setTimeout so interval adapts in real-time
- Prevents concurrent pushes with framePushBusy guard
- Logs FPS changes for diagnostics

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Merge fix/chatbot-and-slides-sync and claude/keen-hellman into main (#8)

* fix: wire chatbot live context to actual regime classifier pipeline

The chatbot was querying an empty regime_states table instead of using
the same classifier pipeline as the dashboard, causing it to always
report "unknown" regime. Now fetch_live_context() uses
get_latest_features() + MLRegimeClassifier (with rule-based fallback),
matching exactly what the dashboard endpoint computes.

- Rewrite fetch_live_context() to mirror /api/v1/regime/current logic
- Add VIX level, volatility regime, and regime probabilities to context
- Compute recent transitions from actual CISS/VIX data (not empty table)
- Reduce context cache TTL from 15min to 2min
- Update system prompt to tell Claude the context is live dashboard data
- Update Python schemas and TypeScript types to match enriched context

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add capstone presentation with 11 slides, speaker notes, and figures

Includes build_presentation.py (python-pptx), speaker_notes.md,
generated .pptx, 5 research figures, and 3 dashboard screenshots.

commit.message:
feat: version info (--version/-v) and self-verification command (verify)

- mvc-inspect -v / --version: displays version, runtime, OS, product links
- mvc-inspect verify: 21 automated checks across 5 categories
  - Assembly Integrity: version, SHA-256 hash, Roslyn dependency
  - Security Guard: protected extensions, path guards, timestamps, gitignore
  - Runtime Compatibility: .NET version, UTF-8, SHA-256 crypto, FS access
  - Dependency Integrity: assembly resolution, Roslyn version
  - File System Safety: system dir detection, temp access, path traversal
- No access keys or credentials required — fully local verification
- 103 unit tests (10 new for SelfVerifier)
- Bump to v2.8.0

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

chore(linter,formatter): Add dev build commands to Justfile for oxlint and oxfmt. (#20106)

These build significantly faster for me while letting me still test the
app locally. I remember discussing these a while ago, but we never added
anything to the Justfile for easily using them.

I'm not sure if there are any downsides to this beyond potential
edge-case differences from the release build, but I figure it's probably
worth considering?

commit.message:
Session kind, pre-dispatch ping, busy detection, swarm timeout

Session classification:
- Add SessionKind type: interactive | agent | swarm | scheduled
- Add kind field to SessionMeta with auto-migration from legacy fields
- Set kind on creation (agent-tools, swarm-tool, schedule-manager)
- Unobserved tracker uses kind instead of isSwarmSession
- Both session UIs filter swarm sessions, sort by kind then MRU

Reliability:
- Pre-dispatch SDK ping with 5s timeout, resets stale client
- Two-phase watchdog: 60s for first event, 5min between events
- Detect busy state from incoming live events (remote dispatches)
- Swarm timeout increased to 15 minutes

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: add characterAssetsLoaded handler to render PNG sprite sheets

The webview had no handler for the characterAssetsLoaded message type
sent by the extension host. This caused the characterSheets Map to
never be populated, so drawCharacterFromSheet() always returned false
and all characters fell back to inline colored-rectangle sprites.

Changes:
- assetLoader.ts: Add loadCharacterSheetsFromUris() that loads PNGs
  via Image(), strips baked backgrounds with removeBackground(), and
  computes frame dimensions matching the existing loadAssets() logic
- useExtensionMessages.ts: Add characterAssetsLoaded case that calls
  the new loader via dynamic import
- Add console.log diagnostics to all asset loading paths for debugging

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: add proactive-watchers skill - URL/API monitoring with AI triggers

Multi-runtime skill (Claude/Copilot/Gemini) that polls URLs and APIs
at configurable intervals and triggers AI agent prompts when conditions
are met.

Core features:
- 7 condition types: value_changed, new_item, status_code_change,
  text_contains, value_exceeds, regex_match, content_hash_changed
- 4 trigger methods: background_task, shell, webhook, log_only
- State persistence with JSONL event history
- Exponential backoff on errors
- Concurrent watchers via thread pool
- Template variable substitution in prompts
- Zero external dependencies (stdlib + curl)

Includes 4 example templates: github_releases, price_monitor,
website_status, api_monitor.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(bot): remove Nominatim, use AOAI for all geocoding

- geocoding.ts deleted — Nominatim dependency gone entirely
- aoai.ts: LocationInfo type now lives here; updated system prompts
  return country+state in same call as coordinates (no follow-up needed);
  added reverseGeocodeWithAoai(lat, lng) for EXIF GPS path
- pinProcessor.ts: imports simplified; EXIF path uses reverseGeocodeWithAoai;
  message text passed verbatim to AOAI (no Nominatim-style pre-parsing needed)
- importHandler.ts: failure report now shows @username alongside message link

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(image-to-3d): add TripoSR CPU backend for true 3D reconstruction

Previously, when no GPU or cloud API keys were available, the skill
fell back to depth-anything which produced flat 2D depth reliefs
instead of true 3D models (Z/X ratio ~5.8%).

Changes:
- Add triposr_cpu backend that runs TripoSR directly on CPU
- Add PyMCubes-based torchmcubes shim (CUDA not required)
- Update converter backend selection: GPU → cloud → CPU TripoSR
- Update fallback chain to include triposr_cpu

Results with Snorty pig image:
- True volumetric mesh: 73K vertices, 147K faces
- Watertight geometry, Euler number 2
- Z/X ratio: 77% (vs 5.8% with depth-anything)
- Vertex colors preserved
- Exports to GLB/OBJ/STL successfully

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add fail-fast stage-gate checks to compiler pipeline (#614)

* Initial plan

* Add fail-fast stage-gate checks with central invariant module

Introduce src/emx_onnx_cgen/invariants.py with four gate checks:
- check_graph_integrity: Import-Gate validates graph structure and
  non-negative tensor dimensions
- check_lowered_ops: Lowering-Gate validates each op has output names
- check_inferred_types: Type-Gate validates dtypes are resolvable
- check_inferred_shapes: Shape-Gate validates non-negative output shapes

Integrate gates into compiler.py pipeline at each phase boundary.
Add 18 unit tests in tests/test_invariants.py.

Co-authored-by: strimo378 <59825937+strimo378@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address code review: clarify error handling in gate checks

Add explicit docstring explanations and inline comments explaining
that ShapeInferenceError propagates uncaught as a genuine gate failure,
while UnsupportedOpError is caught for non-tensor outputs only.

Co-authored-by: strimo378 <59825937+strimo378@users.noreply.github.com>

* tests: update golden references

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: strimo378 <59825937+strimo378@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Re-rank MusicBrainz search results by artist match and release type (#335)

* Initial plan

* Re-rank MusicBrainz results to prioritize artist matches and entity type

For searchReleases: artist matches ranked first, then Album type preferred,
then by MusicBrainz score. For searchRecordings: artist matches ranked first,
then non-live recordings, then by score.

Co-authored-by: tjnicolaides <1401894+tjnicolaides@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add edge case test for releases with missing artist-credit

Co-authored-by: tjnicolaides <1401894+tjnicolaides@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add granular release type ranking with preferredType parameter

Replace binary Album-vs-everything sort with a proper type hierarchy:
preferredType (rank 0) > Album > EP > Single > Broadcast > Compilation/Soundtrack.

searchReleases now accepts an optional preferredType param (defaults to
'Album') so callers can specify Single, EP, etc. when appropriate.

* Implement the musicbrainz weight changes into the component

commit.message:
Build EduCorps Phase 1 backend & upgrade Next.js to patch DoS CVE (#3)

* Initial plan

* feat: Add complete Next.js 14 backend — auth, AI grading, flashcards, marketplace, and more

- Add Supabase client/server/admin clients
- Add all TypeScript interfaces (types/index.ts)
- Add AES-256-CBC encryption for Groq API keys (lib/crypto/encryptKey.ts)

diveopt 8: fix JustOne when_ propagation to inner dive heuristics

CbcHeuristicJustOne::addHeuristic() always stores inner heuristics with
when_=-999 (forcing them to run when selected, bypassing the probability
gate). This is correct for normal use but inadvertently discards any
outer mode information such as diveopt=8.

Fix: in CbcHeuristicJustOne::solution(), temporarily set the inner
heuristic's when_ to the outer JustOne's when_ before calling its
solution() method, then restore -999 immediately after. This lets
mode-specific logic inside the dive (e.g. diveopt 8 aggressive
feasibility mode) see the correct setting without affecting the gate
bypass behaviour.

Also add a comment in CbcSolverHeuristics.cpp explaining why individual
inner heuristics in the DivingS JustOne container no longer need an
explicit setWhen() call.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: illuminate content pass — three-phase corpus narrative (v4.5.2)

Illuminate global mode now returns actual content atoms instead of tag stubs.

Three-phase architecture:
1. globalTopNodes(): weighted degree centrality → top mem_ compound hubs
2. bfsViaEdges(): reaches dominant tag atoms (concept spine)
3. fetchContentAtomsByTopTags(): pulls content atoms ranked by thematic
   centrality (how many top-hub tags each atom shares)

An atom tagged #rust + #code + #anchor scores higher than one with only
#rust — the output is the most cross-thematic real passages in the corpus.

With auto_budget: 350M-char corpus → ~350K chars output (1000:1).
Content atoms already sorted by centrality; no edge-degree rerank needed.

Bug fixes:
- mem_ IDs now filtered from BFS result before fetchNodes
- Budget trim no longer guarded by edges.length > 0 (illuminate clears edges)

Docs:
- Standard 128 → v2.0: full explore/illuminate semantic split
- Search_Protocol.md section 5 rewritten
- CHANGELOG v4.5.2 entry

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: tileset assets not loading — assetsReady never set, legacy data keys mismatched

Two bugs prevented custom tileset assets from rendering:

1. setTilesetMetadata() loaded the tileset PNG and built metadata indexes
   but never set assetsReady=true. The renderer gates all PNG rendering
   behind areAssetsReady(), so it always fell back to inline sprites.

2. The extension only sent tilesetMetadataLoaded (with metadata item IDs
   like 'desk_work_monitor') OR tilesetAssetsLoaded (with tileset.json
   names like 'work_desk_v1'), never both. The renderer's
   furnitureToTileset mapping uses tileset.json names, so metadata-only
   mode produced an empty object lookup.

Fixes:
- setTilesetMetadata() now sets assetsReady=true on image load
- New setLegacyTilesetAssets() function handles tilesetAssetsLoaded
- Extension always sends BOTH metadata + legacy messages when available
- Webview now handles tilesetAssetsLoaded message type

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Phase 4.3: Implement Intel EU backend compiler

Implement the main compile() function in the EU backend that ties together
the existing register allocator, instruction lowering, and binary encoding
infrastructure.

Implementation:
- Complete EUCompiler::compile() method that was previously a placeholder
- Add statement lowering for: Emit, Store, Return, If, Loop, Block
- Add expression lowering for: Binary, Unary, Math, Select operations
- Generate binary EU machine code from naga IR
- Add EOT (end-of-thread) marker to compiled binaries

The compiler now successfully compiles basic WGSL shaders to Intel EU
machine code binary format. Advanced features like texture sampling,
URB I/O, and optimization passes are deferred to future work.

Testing:
- All 153 Rust tests pass (including 2 new EU compilation tests)
- All Go tests pass (no regressions)
- Basic vertex shader compiles to valid binary kernel

Phase 4.3 Status: Core compilation pipeline ✅ FUNCTIONAL
Next work: URB I/O handling, texture SEND instructions, optimization

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: 35 tests, todo example, reactive hooks, CSS fix, warning cleanup

Tests (5 → 35 across workspace):
  oxide-core (19 tests):
    - signal: read/write, update, display, copy semantics, add/sub assign
    - effects: tracking, resubscribe on condition, multiple per signal
    - memo: computed values, chained memos
    - batch: nested batch coalescing
    - untrack: prevents subscription
    - context: provide/use, overwrite
    - watch: skips initial, receives new value
    - hooks: fire on signal operations
  oxide-router (10 tests):
    - exact match, no match, param match, multiple params
    - trailing slashes, root route, different segments/length
  oxide-resiliency (6 tests):
    - RetryConfig builders, CircuitState display
    - CircuitError/RetryError/TimeoutError display

New example: examples/todo
  - Full TodoMVC-style app (170 lines)
  - Add/toggle/delete todos, filter (all/active/completed)
  - localStorage persistence, clear completed

Bug fix: watch() self-referential cycle
  - Used untrack() when reading prev signal to prevent
    the effect from subscribing to its own state signal

CSS fix: CLI install line no longer scrolls on desktop
  - Removed max-width: 600px constraint
  - Added overflow-x: auto + font-size: 0.85rem

Warning cleanup: removed unused imports across router/resiliency

commit.message:
refactor: complete CombatEngine decomposition — status effects, abilities, logging (#1205) (#1222)

* refactor: complete CombatEngine decomposition — status effects, abilities, logging (#1205)

Pass A — StatusEffectApplicator:
- Migrate ApplyOnDeathEffects (CursedZombie on-death curse, PlagueBear
  poison-on-death) to StatusEffectApplicator
- Migrate CheckOnDeathEffects (ArchlichSovereign revive mechanic) to
  StatusEffectApplicator
- Implement ResetCombatEffects in StatusEffectApplicator (clears status
  effects, player combat flags, passive state) — replaces inline block
  in HandleLootAndXP
- CombatEngine retains thin private wrappers that delegate to the service

Pass B — AbilityProcessor:
- Add SetStats(RunStats) to IAbilityProcessor (mirrors AttackResolver
  pattern) so ability damage and usage are tracked correctly
- Migrate HandleAbilityMenu (silence check, cooldown/mana classification,
  menu display, ability execution, stat tracking) to AbilityProcessor
- Migrate HandleItemMenu (consumable filter, menu display, UseItem call)
  to AbilityProcessor
- CombatEngine.RunCombat now calls _abilityProcessor.SetStats(_stats)
  alongside the existing _attackResolver.SetStats(_stats)

Pass C — CombatLogger:
- Migrate ColorizeDamage and its ReplaceLastOccurrence helper to
  CombatLogger; all call sites in CombatEngine delegate to the service
- Migrate ShowDeathNarration (boss vs regular enemy death text) to
  CombatLogger
- Migrate ShowRecentTurns (last-3-turns display) to CombatLogger;
  accepts IReadOnlyList<CombatTurn> rather than reading _turnLog directly
- Implement LogTurn as turnLog.Add(turn) in CombatLogger

CombatEngine is now a pure orchestrator: it coordinates the focused
classes but contains no formatting, effect application, or menu logic
itself.

All 1757 tests pass. Zero behavioral change.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* chore: update Coulson history for #1205 decomposition

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(bedrock): strip output_config from Bedrock Invoke requests (#23042)

* fix(bedrock): strip output_config from Bedrock Invoke requests

Bedrock Invoke API does not support the output_config parameter
(added to Anthropic Messages API). Requests with output_config cause
400 errors: 'extraneous key [output_config] is not permitted'.

Strip output_config in both Bedrock Invoke transformation layers
(messages and chat), consistent with how output_format is already
handled and how VertexAI strips both parameters.

Fixes: https://github.com/BerriAI/litellm/issues/22797

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* test(bedrock): add output_config test for chat/invoke path

Addresses review feedback — the chat/invoke_transformations path now has
symmetric test coverage matching the messages/invoke_transformations path.

Fixes: https://github.com/BerriAI/litellm/issues/22797

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: giulio-leone <6887247+giulio-leone@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
chore(opencode): align permissions with non-interactive ACP design

- Set default permission to deny (non-interactive system rejects unknowns)
- Allow read-only tools: read, list, glob, grep, lsp, todoread/write
- Allow core functionality: task, skill, webfetch, websearch, codesearch
- Deny interactive tools: question, edit (matches ChatbotClient rejection)
- Configure bash whitelist for skill scripts and safe utilities
- Restrict external_directory to skills paths and agent workspace only
- Remove ask permissions since AIr-Friends has no interactive prompt

commit.message:
fix(client,types): fix segfault on command - use-after-free in reply()

msg._ptr is a C pointer only valid during the TDLib callback. After the
callback returns TDLib frees the message. Any coroutine scheduled via
run_coroutine_threadsafe that later calls tg_reply_text(msg._ptr) would
dereference freed memory -> segfault.

Fix:
- reply(): replace tg_reply_text(msg._ptr) with send_message(reply_to_id)
  which uses only the already-copied msg.chat_id and msg.id (safe ints)
- types.py Message: remove self._ptr storage with a warning comment
- router.py: fix import TG_FILTER_OUTGOING (was importing INCOMING)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
